```spthy
theory Signed_Token_CertPath_P
begin

/* 
  Model of a signed-and-encrypted token with a certification path.
  Roles:
    - A (Sender): creates a fresh nonce rA and timestamp tA, then sends to B:
        (B -> A) , A{ tA, rA, B, sgnData, Bp[encData] }
      Here, A{...} denotes appending a signature over a hash of the Info payload.
      Bp[encData] = aenc(encData, pkB).

    - B (Receiver): obtains Ap from the certification path (B -> A), verifies A's signature
      over the Info payload, checks that B is the intended recipient, and decrypts encData.

  Cryptographic primitives:
    - Public-key encryption: aenc/adec
    - Digital signatures: sign/verify
    - Hash function: h

  Intended security goals:
    - Secrecy of encData: the adversary should not learn encData unless B's long-term key is revealed.
    - Authentication (non-injective) of A to B: if B accepts a token, then either A sent it before,
      or A's long-term signing key was revealed beforehand.
*/

builtins: asymmetric-encryption, signing, hashing

functions:
  mkpath/2,            // abstract constructor for a certification path "B -> A"
  get_ap/1[destructor] // extract A's public key from the certification path

equations:
  get_ap(mkpath(anchor, pkA)) = pkA

let A(~skA, pkB) =
  /* A constructs the signed token and sends it with a certification path B -> A. */
  new tA;                 // timestamp (abstract)
  new rA;                 // fresh, non-repeating number to prevent replay
  new sgnData;            // non-confidential parameters
  new encData;            // confidential parameters to be protected for B

  let info = <'token', tA, rA, pkB, sgnData, aenc(encData, pkB)> in
  let sig  = sign(h(info), ~skA) in
  let path = mkpath(pkB, pk(~skA)) in          // certification path (B -> A), abstractly yields pkA

  event TokenSent(pk(~skA), pkB, tA, rA, sgnData, encData);
  event SecretData(pk(~skA), pkB, encData);

  out(<path, info, sig>)

let B(~skB) =
  /* B receives the certification path and signed token; verifies and decrypts. */
  in(msg);
  let <path, info, sig> = msg in
  let pkA = get_ap(path) in                    // obtain A's public key from the path B -> A
  if verify(sig, h(info), pkA) = true then
    let <'token', tA, rA, =pk(~skB), sgnData, xenc> = info in  // ensure B is the intended recipient
    let encData = adec(xenc, ~skB) in
    event TokenAccepted(pkA, pk(~skB), tA, rA, sgnData, encData)

process:
!(
  new ~skA; out(pk(~skA));
  !(
    new ~skB; out(pk(~skB));
    (
        !A(~skA, pk(~skB))
      | !B(~skB)
      | (event LtkReveal(pk(~skA)); out(~skA))  // optional compromise of A's signing key
      | (event LtkReveal(pk(~skB)); out(~skB))  // optional compromise of B's decryption key
    )
  )
)

lemma executability:
  exists-trace
  "Ex pkA pkB t r s d #i #j.
      TokenAccepted(pkA, pkB, t, r, s, d) @ #i
    & TokenSent(pkA, pkB, t, r, s, d) @ #j
    & #j < #i"

lemma secrecy_encData:
  "All pkA pkB d #i.
      SecretData(pkA, pkB, d) @ i
    ==> not(Ex #j. K(d) @ j)
        | (Ex #r. LtkReveal(pkB) @ r)"

lemma auth_B_accepts_from_A:
  "All pkA pkB t r s d #i.
      TokenAccepted(pkA, pkB, t, r, s, d) @ i
    ==> (Ex #j. TokenSent(pkA, pkB, t, r, s, d) @ j & j < i)
        | (Ex #r. LtkReveal(pkA) @ r & r < i)"

end
```

begin

/*

    - Authentication (non-injective) of A to B: if B accepts a token, then either A sent it before,
      or A's long-term signing key was revealed beforehand.

  get_ap/1[destructor] // extract A's public key from the certification path

equations:

  new sgnData;            // non-confidential parameters
  new encData;            // confidential parameters to be protected for B

  let info = <'token', tA, rA, pkB, sgnData, aenc(encData, pkB)> in

  let path = mkpath(pkB, pk(~skA)) in          // certification path (B -> A), abstractly yields pkA

  event TokenSent(pk(~skA), pkB, tA, rA, sgnData, encData);
  event SecretData(pk(~skA), pkB, encData);

  out(<path, info, sig>)

    let <'token', tA, rA, =pk(~skB), sgnData, xenc> = info in  // ensure B is the intended recipient
    let encData = adec(xenc, ~skB) in

!(

  !(

    (

      | !B(~skB)

      | (event LtkReveal(pk(~skB)); out(~skB))  // optional compromise of B's decryption key
    )
  )
)

  exists-trace
  "Ex pkA pkB t r s d #i #j.
      TokenAccepted(pkA, pkB, t, r, s, d) @ #i
    & TokenSent(pkA, pkB, t, r, s, d) @ #j
    & #j < #i"

  "All pkA pkB d #i.
      SecretData(pkA, pkB, d) @ i

  "All pkA pkB t r s d #i.
      TokenAccepted(pkA, pkB, t, r, s, d) @ i
    ==> (Ex #j. TokenSent(pkA, pkB, t, r, s, d) @ j & j < i)

```spthy
theory Signed_Token_CertPath_P
begin

/* 
  Model of a signed-and-encrypted token with a certification path.

  Roles:
    - A (Sender): creates a fresh nonce rA and timestamp tA, then sends to B:
        (B -> A) , A{ tA, rA, B, sgnData, Bp[encData] }
      Here, A{...} denotes appending a signature over a hash of the Info payload.
      Bp[encData] = aenc(encData, pkB).

    - B (Receiver): obtains Ap from the certification path (B -> A), verifies A's signature
      over the Info payload, checks that B is the intended recipient, and decrypts encData.

  Cryptographic primitives:
    - Public-key encryption: aenc/adec
    - Digital signatures: sign/verify
    - Hash function: h

  Intended security goals:
    - Secrecy of encData: the adversary should not learn encData unless B's long-term key is revealed.
    - Authentication (non-injective) of A to B: if B accepts a token, then either A sent it before,
      or A's long-term signing key was revealed beforehand.
*/

builtins: asymmetric-encryption, signing, hashing

let A(~skA, pkB) =
  /* A constructs the signed token and sends it with a certification path B -> A. */
  new tA;                 // timestamp (abstract)
  new rA;                 // fresh, non-repeating number to prevent replay
  new sgnData;            // non-confidential parameters
  new encData;            // confidential parameters to be protected for B

  let info = <'token', tA, rA, pkB, sgnData, aenc(encData, pkB)> in
  let sig  = sign(h(info), ~skA) in
  let path = <'cpath', pkB, pk(~skA)> in          // abstract certification path (B -> A)

  event TokenSent(pk(~skA), pkB, tA, rA, sgnData, encData);
  event SecretData(pk(~skA), pkB, encData);

  out(<path, info, sig>)

let B(~skB) =
  /* B receives the certification path and signed token; verifies and decrypts. */
  in(msg);
  let <path, info, sig> = msg in
  let <'cpath', pkB_path, pkA> = path in          // obtain A's public key from the path B -> A
  if pkB_path = pk(~skB) then
    if verify(sig, h(info), pkA) = true then
      let <'token', tA, rA, pkB_info, sgnData, xenc> = info in
      if pkB_info = pk(~skB) then                 // ensure B is the intended recipient
        let encData = adec(xenc, ~skB) in
        event TokenAccepted(pkA, pk(~skB), tA, rA, sgnData, encData)

process:
!(
  new ~skA; out(pk(~skA));
  !(
    new ~skB; out(pk(~skB));
    (
        !A(~skA, pk(~skB))
      | !B(~skB)
      | (event LtkReveal(pk(~skA)); out(~skA))  /* optional compromise of A's signing key */
      | (event LtkReveal(pk(~skB)); out(~skB))  /* optional compromise of B's decryption key */
    )
  )
)

lemma executability:
  exists-trace
  "Ex pkA pkB t r s d #i #j.
      TokenAccepted(pkA, pkB, t, r, s, d) @ #i
    & TokenSent(pkA, pkB, t, r, s, d) @ #j
    & #j < #i"

lemma secrecy_encData:
  "All pkA pkB d #i.
      SecretData(pkA, pkB, d) @ i
    ==> not(Ex #j. K(d) @ j)
        | (Ex #r. LtkReveal(pkB) @ r)"

lemma auth_B_accepts_from_A:
  "All pkA pkB t r s d #i.
      TokenAccepted(pkA, pkB, t, r, s, d) @ i
    ==> (Ex #j. TokenSent(pkA, pkB, t, r, s, d) @ j & j < i)
        | (Ex #r. LtkReveal(pkA) @ r & r < i)"

end
